bullets.text: The US has charged Cuba's Raúl Castro and five others with murder and conspiracy over the 1996 downing of two planes, raising tensions between the two nations.
Ebola vaccine development could take up to nine months, as the World Health Organization reports 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases in the latest outbreak.
Russia and China showcased their unity on the world stage during President Putin's visit to Xi Jinping, though no pipeline deal was secured.
A photographer has been charged with allegedly stealing a camera from a victim of the Bondi terror attack and later pawning it.
The ATO has issued an apology after a 97-year-old woman was fined for not prioritizing tax obligations following her husband's death, drawing criticism from the ombudsman.
A Victorian teenager has been accused by prosecutors of attempting to hijack a Jetstar plane due to political motivations.

Nvidia announced it would return over $80 billion to shareholders after reporting better-than-expected revenue and forecasts, with the company's CEO also engaging in a $90 billion deal spree to further its AI dominance.
Oil prices dropped nearly 6% and emerging currencies rallied amid hopes for a deal to ease the conflict in the Middle East, which has fueled inflation concerns and rattled financial markets.
SpaceX has filed publicly for its initial public offering on Nasdaq under the symbol SPCX, moving Elon Musk's company closer to what could be the world's biggest-ever debut.
